stimulus: Buildings ranging from the Centre Pompidou in Paris to the Lloyd's headquarters in London to the Pompidou Metz ______blank designed by the Italian-British architect Richard Rogers.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The plural verb "were" agrees in number with the plural subject "buildings."

Choice A is incorrect because the singular verb "has been" doesn't agree in number with the plural subject "buildings." Choice C is incorrect because the singular verb "is" doesn't agree in number with the plural subject "buildings." Choice D is incorrect because the singular verb "was" doesn't agree in number with the plural subject "buildings."
